entities.name: saṃskāra
entities.cite: Manifestation requires that something be made ready

denials.name: utpatti
denials.cite: the word is manifested, not produced

rejected_readings.label: production_utpatti
rejected_readings.rendering: Sounds produce a new word that did not exist before.
rejected_readings.why: Contradicts the doctrine of manifestation: 'the word is manifested, not produced'. Production theories make the word dependent on sounds for creation, but manifestation requires the word to be eternal.

notes: Verse 1.78 doxographically presents three positions of the abhivyakti (manifestation) proponents, differing on whether sounds prepare the sense-organ alone, word alone, or both.
